Multi-Agent System

How Ralph Works

A self-correcting loop that turns your ideas into production-ready code

01

The Core Concept

Think of Ralph as a two-person team that never sleeps

Traditional AI coding tools are like having a conversation with a single assistant. You ask, they answer, you copy-paste, you find bugs, you go back and ask again. It's slow and tedious.

Ralph takes a different approach: it orchestrates two specialized AI agents that work together automatically. The Developer writes code while the Reviewer validates it. They iterate in a loop until the code meets quality standards—without you lifting a finger.

This concept, pioneered by Geoffrey Huntley, mirrors how real development teams work: someone writes, someone reviews, feedback is incorporated, and the cycle continues until everyone is satisfied.

Mental Model

Think of Ralph as hiring a junior developer and a senior reviewer who work together 24/7. You give them the requirements, and they don't stop until the code is ready to ship.

02

The Workflow

From PROMPT.md to committed code in four stages

1

You Write PROMPT.md

Describe what you want in plain English. Include requirements, constraints, and any technical preferences. This is the single source of truth for your task.

2

Developer Agent Writes

The Developer agent reads your prompt, analyzes your codebase, and implements the changes. It has full access to your repository and modifies files directly.

3

Reviewer Agent Validates

The Reviewer examines the code for quality, bugs, security issues, and adherence to your requirements. If problems are found, it sends feedback to the Developer.

4

Commit is Created

Once both agents agree the code is ready, Ralph creates a semantic Git commit. Your changes are ready to review and push.

03

The Self-Correcting Loop

The secret sauce that makes Ralph actually work

The magic of Ralph isn't just having two agents—it's the feedback loop between them.

Developer Writes

The Developer agent produces initial code based on your PROMPT.md

Reviewer Examines

The Reviewer checks for bugs, security issues, code style, and requirement adherence

Feedback is Given

If issues are found, specific feedback is sent back to the Developer

Developer Fixes

The Developer addresses the feedback and produces improved code

Loop Until Approved

This continues until the Reviewer approves or the iteration limit is reached

Iteration Modes

Control how many cycles Ralph runs:

  • -Q Quick — 3 passes
  • -S Standard — 5 passes
  • -T Thorough — 10 passes
  • -E Extensive — 20 passes
  • -L Long — 30 passes

More passes = higher quality, but takes longer and costs more in API calls.

04

Before vs After

See the difference Ralph makes in your workflow

Manual AI Chat

Before Ralph
  • ✕ Copy prompt to chat interface
  • ✕ Wait for AI response
  • ✕ Copy code back to editor
  • ✕ Find bugs manually
  • ✕ Go back to chat, explain bug
  • ✕ Repeat until frustrated
  • ✕ Write commit message yourself
Typical time: 30-60 minutes
vs

Ralph Workflow

With Ralph
  • ✓ Write PROMPT.md once
  • ✓ Run ralph -S
  • ✓ Go get coffee ☕
  • ✓ Return to committed code
Typical time: 3-10 minutes

Ready to try it yourself?

Install Ralph in under 5 minutes and run your first automated workflow.

Ralph Workflow

Multi-agent AI orchestration for automated code development. Ship your ideas with autonomous agents that collaborate until quality is achieved.
